Oracle Events
- Search Emeka's inbox using search_emails (query: "Derek Hollis" or "GreenStack renewal" or "GreenStack contract"). Find email_derek_greenstack_renewal_apr2026, Derek's April 22, 2026 email to emeka.diallo@moveops.com raising four pre-renewal concerns: Noah Fitzgerald's confusing paperwork referencing another company, Priya Venkatesh's pending $379 headphones reimbursement, an unapproved $1,650 hazmat storage line item on INV-2026-0418, and a request for GreenStack's full YTD invoice total from CFO Renata Osei.
- Search CRM using crm_search_companies (name: "GreenStack"), crm_search_deals (company_id: "company_greenstack"), and crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_greenstack"]). Find deal_greenstack_apr2026_zhao_reyes (amount: $9,440, dealstage: "inprogress") with description explicitly flagging the $1,650 interim hazmat storage as "PENDING client approval from Derek Hollis," and engagement_greenstack_apr2026_zhao_reyes (NOTE, April 1, 2026) confirming the same under PENDING ITEMS.
- Search Airtable relocations using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Company", value: "GreenStack Energy"). Find six records including recReloc00000006 (Noah Fitzgerald, Philadelphia to Seattle, Status: Completed, Coordinator: Fatimah Al-Rashidi), the record that will conflict with the open Linear tickets found later.
- Search Airtable stipend transactions using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Stipend Transactions", field_name: "Account", value: "GreenStack Energy"). Find recStip000000013: Priya Venkatesh, "Noise-Canceling Headphones," $379, Date: 2026-04-18, Has Receipt: false, Approval Status: "Pending," Review Notes: "Missing receipt. Requested employee resubmit with proof of purchase."
- Search Slack #operations (channel C006) using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "fitzgerald" or "fitzpatrick" or "noah"). Find the February 2026 thread where Emeka tells Fatimah "Fitzpatrick, the Axiom one. Fitzgerald is GreenStack. Easy to mix up," and Fatimah later confirms: "The Axiom/Fitzpatrick intake is complete. I've sent confirmation to Noah, and he's ready for April 14. I updated the Linear ticket with all the details (apartment, flight, Javier on-site)." This is the root cause of Noah Fitzgerald's documentation confusion, Fatimah sent GreenStack's confirmed logistics to Axiom's Noah Fitzpatrick.
- Search Linear using linear_list_issues (project: "proj_greenstack" or query: "Noah Fitzgerald"). Find three open issues: issue_greenstack_fitzgerald_apt (#316, state: in_progress), issue_greenstack_fitzgerald_flight (#317, state: todo), issue_greenstack_fitzgerald_movers (#318, state: todo). All three are open despite Airtable showing Noah Fitzgerald's relocation as "Completed", confirming the documentation inconsistency.
- Search emails using search_emails (query: "hazmat storage" or "storage approval" or "INV-2026-0418") and Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "1650" or "storage approval"). Find no approval email or Slack message from Derek Hollis or GreenStack authorizing the $1,650 charge, confirming the line item was added to INV-2026-0418 without documented written sign-off.
- Search QuickBooks using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria: {CustomerRef: "cust_greenstack"}). Find three 2026 invoices: NMX-2026-0033 ($7,289, Priya Venkatesh, TxnDate 2026-04-04), INV-2026-0303 ($5,000, Tomas Herrera, TxnDate 2026-03-22), INV-2026-0418 ($9,440, Zhao + Reyes including $1,650 pending storage, TxnDate 2026-04-01). YTD 2026 total: $21,729. Note: no QuickBooks invoice yet for Noah Fitzgerald's completed move.
- Search Linear using linear_list_issues (project: "project_dot_compliance_audit" or query: "GreenStack"). Find issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack (state: done), Mei-Lin Zhao's Class 9 lithium-ion battery shipment, all four documentation categories verified, and issue_dot_docs_reyes_greenstack (state: done), Joaquin Reyes's oversize solar rig, documentation complete. Both GreenStack hazmat moves have fully clean DOT compliance records.
- Create a Linear issue using linear_create_issue (team: "team_relocations" or "team_operations", title: referencing Noah Fitzgerald / Axiom documentation error, priority: 1 or 2). Description must detail: (a) Fatimah's move confirmation was sent to Axiom's Noah Fitzpatrick rather than GreenStack's Noah Fitzgerald, (b) Fitzgerald's Linear tickets #316-318 remain open despite Airtable showing "Completed," (c) action required to verify Fitzgerald received correct final logistics confirmation and to close/correct the three open tickets. Tool returns a success response with a new issue ID.
- Create a CRM engagement using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", body: full account review summary, company_ids: ["company_greenstack"]). Body should document all findings: Noah documentation discrepancy and remediation ticket created, Priya stipend pending receipt, $1,650 storage charge unapproved (pending Derek's decision), YTD total $21,729, DOT compliance fully clean, Q3 expansion of 4-5 relocations underway. Tool returns a success response.
- Send a reply email using send_email (sender: "emeka.diallo@moveops.com", recipients: ["derek.hollis@greenstackenergy.com"], content: covering all four concerns). Email must address: (a) Noah, Fatimah's confirmation was sent to Axiom's Noah Fitzpatrick; Fitzgerald's Linear tickets #316-318 remain open despite "Completed" in Airtable; corrective action ticket has been created. (b) Priya, $379 headphones reimbursement is pending because no receipt was attached; she needs to resubmit with proof of purchase. (c) $1,650 storage line item, no formal written approval from GreenStack found; Derek's choice to remove it or provide retroactive sign-off. (d) YTD total, $21,729 across three invoices: NMX-2026-0033 ($7,289), INV-2026-0303 ($5,000), INV-2026-0418 ($9,440). Email should also confirm DOT compliance is fully clean and close with readiness to discuss Q3 expansion. Tool returns a success response.
- Post a summary to #customer-engagement using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C002", payload: summary message). Message should cover: GreenStack renewal inquiry from Derek Hollis, key issues addressed (Noah documentation error + corrective ticket created, Priya stipend pending receipt, $1,650 storage approval TBD with Derek), YTD total $21,729, DOT compliance clean, Q3 expansion interest of approximately 4-5 relocations, and reply sent to Derek. Tool returns a success response

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 The model must use a CRM tool (e.g., crm_search_companies, crm_search_deals, or crm_list_engagements) to look up the GreenStack Energy account.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called crm_search_companies with 'GreenStack', crm_get_company with 'company_greenstack', and crm_list_engagements with company_ids: ['company_greenstack']. CRM was clearly consulted.
✓Run #2The agent called crm_search_companies with 'GreenStack', crm_search_deals with company_id 'company_greenstack', and crm_list_engagements with company_ids ['company_greenstack']. Multiple CRM tools were used to look up the GreenStack account.
✓Run #3The agent called crm_search_companies with name 'GreenStack', crm_search_deals with company_id 'company_greenstack', and crm_list_engagements with company_ids ['company_greenstack'] — all returning results.
✓Run #4The agent called crm_search_companies with 'GreenStack', crm_search_deals with company_id 'company_greenstack', and crm_list_engagements with company_ids ['company_greenstack']. Multiple CRM tools were used to look up the GreenStack Energy account.
✓Run #5The agent called crm_search_companies with name 'GreenStack' and received a successful result with the GreenStack Energy account (id: company_greenstack). It also called crm_search_deals and crm_list_engagements with company_greenstack.
✓Run #6The agent called crm_search_companies with name 'GreenStack' (returning company_greenstack), and also called crm_search_deals and crm_list_engagements with the GreenStack company ID. CRM was clearly consulted as the authoritative source.
2 The model replies to Derek Hollis by either (a) calling send_email with '[derek.hollis@greenstackenergy.com](mailto:derek.hollis@greenstackenergy.com)' in the recipient or recipients parameter, or (b) calling reply_to_email on Derek's inbound email (email_id: 'email_derek_greenstack_renewal_apr2026'), which automatically addresses the reply to Derek.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called reply_to_email with email_id 'email_derek_greenstack_renewal_apr2026', which automatically addresses the reply to Derek Hollis.
✓Run #2The agent called reply_to_email with email_id 'email_derek_greenstack_renewal_apr2026', which satisfies option (b) of the criterion. The reply automatically goes to Derek as the original email sender.
✓Run #3The agent called reply_to_email with email_id 'email_derek_greenstack_renewal_apr2026', which automatically addresses the reply to Derek Hollis.
✓Run #4The agent called reply_to_email with email_id 'email_derek_greenstack_renewal_apr2026', which satisfies option (b) of this criterion. The reply automatically addresses Derek as the recipient.
✓Run #5The agent called reply_to_email with email_id 'email_derek_greenstack_renewal_apr2026', which automatically addresses the reply to Derek Hollis.
✓Run #6The agent called reply_to_email with email_id 'email_derek_greenstack_renewal_apr2026', which automatically addresses the reply to Derek. This satisfies option (b) of the criterion.
3 The reply email to Derek Hollis is successfully sent (i.e., the send_email or reply_to_email tool call returns a success response).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The reply_to_email tool returned a success response with {"email_id": "0ae5f6daecbf4d8b959cc5206cbb0989"}, confirming the email was sent successfully.
✓Run #2The reply_to_email tool returned a success response with {"email_id": "ea037746d3f945348d83b33211c74504"}, confirming the email was sent successfully.
✓Run #3The reply_to_email call returned a success response: {"email_id": "debf361c3ba3470da5564b65249d90de"}, confirming the email was sent successfully.
✓Run #4The reply_to_email call returned a success response with a new email_id: 'd2ead3dc429c430aab5839a7776c6101', confirming the email was successfully sent.
✓Run #5The reply_to_email call returned a success response with email_id 'ed0cfc37c9674b619ccf2006639b93ba'.
✓Run #6The reply_to_email tool returned a success response: {"email_id": "82bce8e8c836489ab49304b10ade46b7"}, confirming the email was successfully sent.
4 The email reply content (send_email or reply_to_email content/body parameter) acknowledges that Noah Fitzgerald's move documentation contained errors or inconsistencies and that MoveOps has identified the issue, such as referencing open or unresolved internal tracking items, paperwork belonging to another relocation, or a mismatch between move records and actual status.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email content explicitly states 'Noah's flight was booked for April 14 instead of his requested date of April 27' and that 'the confirmation email he received contained incorrect details,' and that 'the confirmation email... may have pulled details from another relocation in our system.' This acknowledges documentation errors and a mismatch/paperwork from another relocation.
✓Run #2The email explicitly states 'we've confirmed that Noah's move confirmation email contained incorrect details' and references that the confirmation 'likely pulled details from a concurrent relocation we were coordinating at the same time,' satisfying the criterion's 'paperwork belonging to another relocation' condition.
✓Run #3The email explicitly states Noah received 'a confirmation email containing another relocatee's details' and that 'the error was in the confirmation document that was generated and sent to him,' acknowledging the documentation inconsistency and referencing an internal operations ticket created.
✓Run #4The email content states 'You were right to flag this. We identified the issue: Noah's flight was mistakenly booked for April 14 instead of his requested date of April 27. That incorrect flight date is likely what caused the confirmation to look off.' This clearly acknowledges documentation errors and that MoveOps identified the issue.
✓Run #5The email reply acknowledges that 'something went wrong' with Noah's paperwork, explains the flight was booked for the wrong date and that this made the documentation 'look like it belonged to someone else's move.' It mentions the error caused confusion. However, it does not reference Linear tickets #316, #317, #318 as the rubric evidence mentions, but the criterion title only requires acknowledging errors/inconsistencies and that MoveOps identified the issue, which is met.
✓Run #6The email content explicitly acknowledges Noah Fitzgerald's documentation errors: 'his flight was booked for April 14 instead of his requested date of April 27' and 'The confirmation Noah received likely reflected this incorrect booking, which would explain why the dates and details looked off.' The criterion allows for referencing paperwork belonging to another relocation or mismatches, which is covered here.
5 The email reply content (send_email or reply_to_email content/body parameter) explains the root cause of Noah Fitzgerald's documentation confusion: that his move confirmation was sent to the wrong recipient due to a name mix-up with another client's employee, or that the confirmation he received contained details belonging to a different relocation (or similar)
outcome
 ✗✓✗✗✗✗ 17%
✗Run #1The agent searched Slack for 'Noah confirmation wrong' and 'Noah paperwork' but failed to find the two-Noahs mix-up (confirmation sent to Axiom's Noah Fitzpatrick). The email describes the root cause as a possible template/mail-merge error pulling wrong data — not that the confirmation was sent to the wrong recipient. The Fitzpatrick/Axiom mix-up is never mentioned.
✓Run #2The email states 'the confirmation he received likely pulled details from a concurrent relocation we were coordinating at the same time,' which satisfies 'the confirmation he received contained details belonging to a different relocation (or similar)' as specified in the criterion.
✗Run #3The email describes the error as a 'system-level error' where Noah received 'another relocatee's details,' but never identifies the specific root cause — that the confirmation was sent to the wrong Noah (Fitzpatrick at Axiom Precision Manufacturing instead of Fitzgerald at GreenStack). The agent searched Slack but did not find or disclose the two-Noahs name mix-up.

12 The email reply content (send_email or reply_to_email content/body parameter) offers Derek Hollis a resolution path for the unapproved $1,650 interim hazmat storage charge on invoice INV-2026-0418, specifically, stating that GreenStack Energy can either have the charge removed or provide retroactive written sign-off to authorize it.
outcome

If Renata or you can sign off on it, we'll update the invoice accordingly.' It does not offer the alternative of removing the charge. The criterion requires both options (removal or retroactive sign-off) to be presented.
✓Run #2The email explicitly offers two options: (1) 'Issue a revised invoice with the $1,650 removed, and bill it separately once she approves' or (2) 'Keep the invoice as-is and have her provide written approval to formalize it,' directly matching the criterion.

✗Run #4The email only offers one resolution path: 'could you or Renata provide written confirmation that GreenStack approves the $1,650 interim hazmat storage line item?' It does not offer the alternative of removing the charge from the invoice. Both options (removal or retroactive sign-off) were required per the criterion.
✓Run #5The email offers two explicit options: 'Option A: We remove the $1,650 line item from INV-2026-0418 and reissue the invoice at $7,790' or 'Option B: If Renata is comfortable approving the charge now, she can send written confirmation.' Both removal and retroactive approval paths are offered.
✓Run #6The email offers both resolution paths: (1) 'could you or Renata reply to this email confirming approval of the $1,650 interim hazmat storage charge?' (retroactive sign-off) and (2) 'if you'd like us to issue a revised invoice without that line item (bringing the total to $7,790), we can do that as well' (charge removal).
